name: Cleanup background worker (retention-based deletion)
description: Background worker that deletes routes (and their files on disk) that are older than the configured retention window AND not marked preserved. Runs on a daily tick by default. Safety rails: dry-run mode by default on first start, a hard cap of N deletions per run, and structured logging of every deletion so operators can audit.
acceptance_criteria: internal/worker/cleanup.go defines CleanupWorker { Queries *db.Queries; Storage *storage.Storage; Settings *settings.Store; Interval time.Duration; MaxDeletionsPerRun int; DryRun bool }

CleanupWorker.Run(ctx) loops on Interval (default 24h); each pass: resolve effective retention_days; if 0, skip; otherwise query routes WHERE preserved=false AND end_time < now() - interval * retention_days, ordered by end_time asc, limited to MaxDeletionsPerRun
For each stale route: delete files under <STORAGE_PATH>/<dongle_id>/<route_name>/ via the storage layer, then delete the routes row (ON DELETE CASCADE handles segments/trips)
A DELETE_DRY_RUN env var (default 'true') makes the worker log what it would delete without touching anything. Setting it to 'false' enables real deletion
Each deletion logs dongle_id, route_name, end_time, bytes_freed at info level
cmd/server/main.go wires up the worker behind CLEANUP_ENABLED env var (default true)

internal/worker/cleanup_test.go uses a real Postgres + tmp STORAGE_PATH: create a route with fake files older than retention, run once, verify files gone and row deleted; verify preserved routes are skipped; verify dry-run does not delete
go vet ./... and go build ./... pass cleanly
notes: Implemented CleanupWorker in internal/worker/cleanup.go with Queries/Storage/Settings/Interval/MaxDeletionsPerRun/DryRun fields, plus EnvRetentionDays for the settings fallback and an overridable now() for deterministic tests. CLEANUP_ENABLED (default true) and DELETE_DRY_RUN (default true) gate the worker in cmd/server/main.go. Added ListStaleRoutes and DeleteRoute via sqlc; segments/trips/events already cascade on route delete. Storage gained RouteDir/RouteBytes/RemoveRoute (with path-traversal guard). Tests apply sql/migrations/*.up.sql into a per-test schema against a real Postgres+PostGIS instance (gated on TEST_DATABASE_URL so CI without a DB still passes) and cover stale delete, preserved skip, dry-run no-op, retention=0 no-op, MaxDeletionsPerRun cap, and empty-result pass. Verified: go test ./... clean, go vet ./... clean, go build ./... clean, gofmt clean. Smoke lint/typecheck failures are pre-existing (script runs eslint/tsc at repo root instead of web/) and unrelated.
